libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated JVM tests and native-image build args with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` when exercising injector construction, interception, or cglib proxy paths. Avoid copying old 2.1.7 tests that import unprefixed internal classes such as `MapMaker`, `MethodProxy`, or `FinalizableReferenceQueue`; in 2.9.1 those shaded internals are renamed with leading `$`, and public Guice API coverage is safer.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Tests that target old internal implementation classes from 2.1.7 will fail javac against 2.9.1 because the jarjar-shaded internal class names changed.
Without the `--add-opens` JVM/native-image argument, injector/proxy paths can fail at runtime with `InaccessibleObjectException` on modern JDKs.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed; the artifact already carries its required Guice core dependencies transitively. Meaningful Guice injector/AOP/proxy coverage on modern JDKs needs java.lang opened because sisu-guice 2.9.1 uses shaded cglib reflection against ClassLoader.defineClass.
